From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

2018 American slasher film

| The Ranger | |
| --- | --- |
| Promotional release poster | |
| Directed by | Jenn Wexler |
| Written by | * + Giaco Furino   + Jenn Wexler |
| Produced by | * + Heather Buckley   + [Larry Fessenden](/wiki/Larry_Fessenden "Larry Fessenden")   + Ashleigh Snead   + Andrew van den Houten   + Jenn Wexler |
| Starring | * + [Chloë Levine](/wiki/Chlo%C3%AB_Levine "Chloë Levine")   + Jeremy Holm   + Amanda Grace Benitez   + Granit Lahu   + [Jeremy Pope](/wiki/Jeremy_Pope_(actor) "Jeremy Pope (actor)")   + Bubba Weiler |
| Cinematography | James Siewert |
| Edited by | * + Abbey Killheffer   + Jenn Wexler |
| Music by | * + [Wade MacNeil](/wiki/Wade_MacNeil "Wade MacNeil")   + [Andrew Gordon Macpherson](/wiki/Andrew_Gordon_Macpherson "Andrew Gordon Macpherson") |
| Production company | * + Hood River Entertainment   + [Glass Eye Pix](/wiki/Glass_Eye_Pix "Glass Eye Pix") |
| Distributed by | [Shudder](/wiki/Shudder_(streaming_service) "Shudder (streaming service)") |
| Release date | * March 12, 2018 (2018-03-12) ([SXSW](/wiki/South_by_Southwest "South by Southwest")) |
| Running time | 77 minutes |
| Country | United States |
| Language | English |

***The Ranger*** is a 2018 American [slasher film](/wiki/Slasher_film "Slasher film") written and directed by Jenn Wexler. It stars [Chloë Levine](/wiki/Chlo%C3%AB_Levine "Chloë Levine") as a [punk](/wiki/Punk_subculture "Punk subculture") who flees with her friends from the law to hide out at her late uncle's cabin in a [national forest](/wiki/National_forest_(United_States) "National forest (United States)"), and Jeremy Holm as the titular [park ranger](/wiki/Park_ranger "Park ranger") who responds in an unhinged manner. Wexler has described the concept of the film as an attempt to "blend the genres of 80s [punk movies](/wiki/List_of_punk_films "List of punk films") with 80s slashers".[[1]](#cite_note-muchado-1) *The Ranger* received mostly positive reviews from the critics, praised for its style and Wexler's direction.

## Plot summary

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Ranger_(film)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot summary")]

After Chelsea and her friends find themselves in trouble with the police, they decide to escape the city and go into hiding. Using a hallucinogenic drug called Echo, they plan to chill out in a secluded family cabin deep in the forest. However, their presence catches the attention of a disturbed park ranger who is determined to preserve the purity of the woods and views the group as a threat. The friends must now struggle to survive against a dangerous, obsessive authority figure.

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Ranger_(film)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Chloë Levine](/wiki/Chlo%C3%AB_Levine "Chloë Levine") as Chelsea
  + Jeté Laurence as young Chelsea
* Jeremy Holm as The Ranger
* Amanda Grace Benitez as Amber
* Granit Lahu as Garth
* [Jeremy Pope](/wiki/Jeremy_Pope_(actor) "Jeremy Pope (actor)") as Jerk
* Bubba Weiler as Abe
* [Larry Fessenden](/wiki/Larry_Fessenden "Larry Fessenden") as Uncle Pete
* Clay McLeod Chapman as Alley cop
* Jim Johnson as Cop
* [Nicholas Tucci](/wiki/Nicholas_Tucci "Nicholas Tucci") as Flesh

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Ranger_(film)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Production")]

The film was based on an old, reworked script from Jenn Wexler's college friend Giaco Furino — they both majored in screenwriting. As the director noted, the original screenplay "was more of a straight up body count movie, with the same overall structure of punks going to the woods and a park ranger starting to take them out."[[1]](#cite_note-muchado-1) The altered story was inspired by punk horror films such as *[The Return of the Living Dead](/wiki/The_Return_of_the_Living_Dead "The Return of the Living Dead")* (1985), as well as the *[Evil Dead](/wiki/Evil_Dead "Evil Dead")* franchise, *[Class of 1984](/wiki/Class_of_1984 "Class of 1984")* (1982), and *[Rituals](/wiki/Rituals_(film) "Rituals (film)")* (1977).[[1]](#cite_note-muchado-1) Jeremy Holm, who was Furino's friend, was cast as The Ranger and was the first cast member attached to the project.[[1]](#cite_note-muchado-1)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Ranger_(film)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Reception")]

*The Ranger* received mostly positive reviews from the critics. On the [review aggregator](/wiki/Review_aggregator "Review aggregator") website [Rotten Tomatoes](/wiki/Rotten_Tomatoes "Rotten Tomatoes"), the film holds a 76% approval rating on based on 37 reviews, with an average rating of 6.9/10.[[2]](#cite_note-2) *[Consequence](/wiki/Consequence_(publication) "Consequence (publication)")'*s Michael Roffman called the film "[pulpy](/wiki/Pulp_magazine "Pulp magazine"), sensational, stylish, and merciless", noting that "Jenn Wexler shows a ton of promise as a filmmaker, and may even have a potential muse in Chloë Levine."[[3]](#cite_note-3) Antol Bitel of *[SciFiNow](/wiki/SciFiNow "SciFiNow")* compared *The Ranger* to [John Boorman](/wiki/John_Boorman "John Boorman")'s *[Deliverance](/wiki/Deliverance "Deliverance")*, writing that it "resonates with hallucinatory flashbacks to cinema's past".[[4]](#cite_note-4) *[ScreenAnarchy](/wiki/ScreenAnarchy "ScreenAnarchy")* editor J Hurtado provided a positive review, calling the film a "fun throwback with a killer soundtrack and enough solid kills in its 77 minutes [...] to sate spiky haired gorehounds everywhere."[[5]](#cite_note-5) Katie Rife from *[The A.V. Club](/wiki/The_A.V._Club "The A.V. Club")* gave the film a "B" score, calling it "a loving tribute to the counterculture tentpoles of punk rock and horror movies". Rife also opined that "Wexler has interpreted her influences thoughtfully and inverted them cleverly. It's the smart kind of dumb fun."[[6]](#cite_note-6) Albert Nowicki of *His Name is Death* praised the film's visuals and setting, and wrote: "The world presented by Wexler is painted in sharp, vivid shades reminiscent of [MTV](/wiki/MTV "MTV")'s music video aesthetics from around forty years ago. Neon lighting contrasts with the raw, chilly landscape of the forest, which resembles the damp woods of the *Evil Dead* rather than the classic summer campgrounds of *[Friday the 13th](/wiki/Friday_the_13th_(franchise) "Friday the 13th (franchise)")*. The cold, autumn wilderness is captured so convincingly, you can almost breathe it in."[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Ranger_(film)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: See also")]

* [List of punk films](/wiki/List_of_punk_films "List of punk films")
